paper.title: Behavior of corrosion damaged circular reinforced concrete columns under cyclic loading

strength_results.notes: Concrete material strength for RC column specimens.

conditions.specimen_geometry: 150 mm x 150 mm x 150 mm cubes

evidence.evidence_text: gth of 820 mm and an span-to-depth ratio ( L/D) of 3.15. Six 150 mm /C2 150 mm /C2 150 mm cubes were cast along with the specimens, and the 28-day mean cube compressive strength was 32.4 MPa. Six 16 mm diame- ter steel bars were used as longitudinal reinforcement, and 8 mm diameter steel bars with a spacing of 100 mm were used as stirrups. For the steel reinforcement, tensile tests were carried out and it was determined that for the 16 mm and 8 mm bars, the yield strength f y were 373.2 MPa and 327 MPa, respectively, and the tensile strength fu were 572.3 MPa and 510.7 MPa, respectively. The controlled variables for this study were corrosion level and
evidence.supports: 28-day mean cube compressive strength of companion cubes was 32.4 MPa
